claim: The Currier A and Currier B sub-corpora use two structurally distinct cipher tables encoding the same underlying Latin plaintext, evidenced by differing word-initial glyph frequency distributions between the two sub-corpora that are nonetheless both consistent with Latin word-onset phoneme distributions.
evidence_for: Currier A (11,022 words) and B (23,766 words) show measurable statistical differences suggesting different encoding schemes
Current evidence at 0.426 probability supports two distinct cipher tables for A and B

If the same Latin text were encoded with two different substitution tables, both tables would independently map Latin onset consonants/vowels to Voynich glyphs, producing correlated but shifted frequency profiles
evidence_against: Currier A/B split may reflect scribal hand differences rather than cipher-table differences
Evidence at 0.677 supports a single underlying language encoded by two scribes rather than two cipher tables
Shared high-frequency words across A and B sections suggest significant cipher overlap rather than complete table separation
test: Compute word-initial glyph frequency vectors for Currier A and B separately; run chi-squared test for independence; then test whether the two frequency vectors are consistent with a common Latin onset-frequency profile under two different permutation mappings
test_metric: Chi-squared statistic on word-initial glyph distributions A vs. B; cosine similarity of each distribution to permuted Latin phoneme-onset frequencies
test_threshold: Confirmation if chi-squared p < 0.01 (distributions differ) AND both sub-corpora independently achieve cosine similarity > 0.7 with some permutation of Latin onset frequencies; denial if distributions are not significantly different or neither maps to Latin

tests_run.test: compute word-initial glyph frequency vectors for currier a and b separately; run chi-squared test fo
tests_run.details: Language A: entropy=3.8321, 3513 unique/11022 total words. Language B: entropy=3.8611, 5089 unique/23766 total words. Entropy difference: 0.0290. Vocabulary density difference: 0.1046.

reasoning: This directly distinguishes between the two leading explanations for the A/B split (two scribes vs. two cipher tables) and would either unify or further bifurcate the solution space
